responses.text: The number of suicides in Japan reached 21,077 in 2020, the first increase in 11 years, and even 2.5 times the number of deaths from COVID-19.
Prime Minister of Japan Yoshihide Suga decided to introduce the post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation.
The number of suicides in Japan fell by 14% during the first wave of the pandemic in February-June 2020
The economic downturn caused by the epidemic has a much greater impact on women than on men.
About 40% of suicide prevention organizations in Japan have temporarily closed or reduced their working hours due to the epidemic
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
The number of suicides among women in Japan increased by almost 15% in 2020.
The number of suicides in October was 2,153, an increase of 39.9%.
The psychological health needs of young people and the psychological impact of the epidemic have become a social problem that cannot be ignored.
The suicide rate in Japan increased by 16% in July-October last year
A long-term economic downturn caused by the epidemic could lead to a rebound in the suicide rate
A large number of women are unemployed during the epidemic, with 280,000, 140,000, and 110,000 fewer women working in the hotel, restaurant, and wholesale and retail industries respectively.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
The suicide rate in 2019 was 16.0, the lowest since 1978, when statistics began
The second wave of the pandemic led to a sharp increase in the number of suicides
The suicide rate among women in Japan increased by more than 70% in one month compared to the same period in 2019.
The number of men in their 20s and 50s who committed suicide in August was 706, an increase of 6.6% over the same period last year.
During the pandemic, people who were 95% close to suicide faced new challenges.
The COVID-19 pandemic has a significant effect on mental health, which could lead to a rise in suicide rates.
The epidemic has exacerbated women's worries about work, childcare, care and domestic violence, according to the Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare.
The COVID-19 epidemic has affected all aspects of Japanese society.
329 suicides occurred in senior classes in 2020.
The “Office of Solitude and Isolation Measures” was established to deal with the serious problem of loneliness and isolation.
Suicides among women in Japan increased significantly in 2020, with 6976 women committing suicide.
There was a 30 percent increase in child suicides from the same time in the previous year.
The increase in mental trauma is one of the reasons for suicide.
Japan's Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare said that according to preliminary data, 20,919 people died by suicide in 2020, an increase of 3.7% over the previous year.
The psychological burden associated with the coronavirus pandemic contributed to the rise in suicide rates.
Local consultation counters have frequently received complaints from women about being unemployed due to the epidemic and being reprimanded by their families.
Japan ’ s October suicides are “more than all Coronavirus deaths this year”
The number of suicides in 2020 reached 20,919, the first increase since the financial crisis in 2009, with 6,976 women accounting for nearly 15% more than in 2019.
The number of female suicide victims in Japan increased by 885 in 2020 compared to the previous year.
The cumulative number of suicides from January to October increased by 160 over the same period last year.
The isolation is unbearable for some people, leading to thoughts of suicide.
The economic problems brought about by COVID-19 contributed to the increase in the number of suicides.
Many women in Japan are employed in casual or non-permanent jobs, making them more vulnerable to economic shocks.
Japan's economic situation has deteriorated due to the impact of the coronavirus epidemic.
For the entire period of 2019, three people suffered from suicide, for the nine months of 2020, these children have already 10.
The suicide rate among women in Japan increased by almost 83 percent in October 2020 compared to last October.
The number of female suicides increased especially, by 83% over the same period, and by 22% compared with the same period for men.
Relevant institutions are investigating this matter, and the suicide of patients due to excessive psychological pressure during isolation has also caused social concern.
Suicide rates in Japan have risen after a decade of declining declines with the COVID-19 epidemic.
Especially housewives are affected by the pandemic
The number of suicides in Japan increased dramatically among women and children during the 2nd wave of the pandemic
237 suicides occurred in senior classes in 2019.
Men committed suicide more often, with 1,320, an increase of 23% in October 2020.
Doctors warn of an extremely serious and dangerous side effect of the pandemic, which is about mental health.
Many faced unemployment, and women had an additional burden at home.
A total of 2,087 people have died from coronavirus in Japan since the start of the year
The number of suicides has increased year-on-year for four consecutive months.
Psychologists believe that the problems that have arisen have become for many the last straw on the way to the decision to commit suicide.
In Japan, women are burdened by the economic crisis as their main fields of employment, including travel and retail jobs, have been hit hard during the quarantine period.
Suicide rates have risen since March and peaked in October.
The social and economic consequences of the pandemic have also led to mental health problems for many Japanese.
The number of suicides has increased year on year, with particularly high rates among women and children.
The suicide rate rose 16 percent from July to October during the second wave of the epidemic.

The spokesman of the Palestinian Authority issued a statement explicitly stating that the Palestinian people strongly condemn the trilateral agreement between the Zionist regime, the United Arab Emirates and the United States.

Bandar bin Sultan criticized Palestinian leaders in the first part of the interview with Al-Arabiya TV, saying that the statements of Palestinian leaders after the UAE and Bahrain agreements with Israel were painful.
Arab League Secretary General Hesam Zaki said that the foreign ministers of Arab League member states rejected the draft Palestinian proposal condemning the normalization of relations with Israel.
The Palestinian Liberation Movement announced that what the residents of the Al-Sheikh Jarrah area are exposed to is the continuation of the occupation plans and the Judaization of Jerusalem.

Mansour Abbas, another Arab MP for the Knesset, stressed that real peace will be achieved with the end of the Israeli occupation and the establishment of an independent Palestinian state in the West Bank and Gaza with Jerusalem as its capital.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas announced that the Palestinians would no longer abide by their agreements with Israel and Washington.

Hamas issued a statement in response to the rejection of the Palestinian proposal to the Arab League, condemning the agreement to normalize relations between the UAE and the Zionist regime, saying that the union has auctioned the Palestinian issue.

Riyad al-Maliki discussed with his Malaysian counterpart the Palestinian diplomatic efforts, especially in the Arab League and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation, in opposition to the normalization of relations between the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain with Israel.
The Palestinian official said the United States had no prior consultation with it and refused to participate in the Israeli-Palestinian Economic Seminar.
The Palestinian leadership rejects any normalization of relations between the Zionist regime and Arab countries before the end of its occupation of Palestinian lands on the 1967 borders.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for an extraordinary meeting of the Arab League as well as an urgent meeting of Palestinian leaders and groups to discuss the consequences of the agreement.
The Palestinian resistance said: "This act of Bahrain was to stab the sacrifices of the Palestinian people, the Islamic Ummah and its martyrs."

Palestine views the normalization of relations between Israel and Bahrain as a weakening of the Arab peace initiative, the decisions of Arab and Islamic summits, and the violation of international law.
Arab countries are divided on the issue of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
Israel has waived the annexation of parts of the Palestinian territories for the time being for a peace treaty with the UAE.
The Arab peace plan in 2002 states that any normalization of relations is contingent on the end of the occupation of Palestine and the solution of the two-state and the principle of peace.

Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas will meet in Ramallah with local Palestinian factions to discuss the growing ties between Israel and Arab states and try to ‘ put an end to the differences ’ between rival groups.
The Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine also said that the signing of a peace agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel is a betrayal and a new conspiracy.
Saudi Arabia’s foreign minister is ready to normalize relations with Israel.

The Arab peace initiative requires Israel to withdraw from the occupied territories of the Palestinian region, including the West Bank and Gaza Strip, as a condition for Arab countries to establish diplomatic relations with Israel.

Palestinian Authority Prime Minister Mohammed Ashtiyah pointed to the agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel to normalize relations and halt the plan for the annexation of the Palestinian territories.

Hani al-Thawbah, a member of the Central Committee of the Palestinian Popular Front, condemned the Moroccan and Zionist regime agreement, saying that any party that deals with relations with the occupiers will give them free service and trade with the blood of the martyrs and the sacrifices of the Palestinian people and the Arab nation.
Bandar bin Sultan said that the boldness of the Palestinian leaders vis-à-vis the Arab states of the Persian Gulf is unacceptable.
Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas reiterated his commitment to a two-state solution based on the 1967 borders with East Jerusalem as its capital.
Mahmoud Abbas is ready for a direct dialogue with Israel
The Palestinian Authority announced that it had summoned the Palestinian ambassador to Bahrain for consultations following Bahrain ’ s move to normalize relations with Israel.
Palestinian Authority calls for a serious peace process with Israel that ends the occupation and blockade of the Gaza Strip and Jerusalem and stops settlement building.
Mahmoud Abbas counts on the mediation of other countries
Palestinians protest against Bahrain's establishment of diplomatic relations with Israel

Peace in the Middle East will come when the United States and Israel recognize the right of the Palestinians to establish an independent state with East Jerusalem as its capital within the borders of June 4, 1967
Egyptian sources spoke with Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas about resuming relations with Israel and returning its ambassadors to the UAE and Bahrain.
The normalization of relations between the Israeli occupation government and the Kingdom of Bahrain is considered a betrayal of Jerusalem, Al-Aqsa Mosque and the Palestinian cause.
The Palestinian Authority is ready to renegotiate with Israel.
Palestinian officials condemned the agreement to normalize relations between Israel and Bahrain, calling it a betrayal of the Palestinian cause.
The official spokesman for the Palestinian Authority reiterated Abbas' opposition to any insult to the ruling figures of any friendly Arab country, including the United Arab Emirates.
The scale of the plastic surgery industry in Korea reached US $10.7 billion in 2020, an increase of 9.2% over 2019.

There are more than 4,000 plastic surgery clinics in South Korea, the world’s largest cosmetic surgery rate, with 13 out of every 1,000 people in the country’s 49 million people having cosmetic surgery.
Many people have taken the opportunity to undergo dental surgery during the epidemic, as wearing a mask makes it less likely to be found that you are undergoing orthodontics.
Medical costs in Malaysia are relatively cheap.
The number of Korean domestic customers has increased significantly in the plastic surgery industry.
The number of foreigners receiving treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals increased by 968 compared with the previous year to 48,849.
Young people in South Korea usually choose to have plastic surgery after the university entrance exam, or consecutive holidays, but the COVID-19 epidemic has broken this norm.
Official complaints about plastic surgery problems and fake doctors doubled in 2013 from a year earlier in South Korea.
The devaluation of the Malaysian currency has reduced medical costs.
The plastic surgery industry in Korea is expected to grow to about US $11.8 billion this year.
Cosmetic hospitals accounted for more than 30 percent of the total cost of foreign patients last year.
South Korea's plastic surgery industry has emerged as a unique hot industry in the epidemic.
Cha Sang-myun, president of the Korean Association of Plastic Surgeons, expressed concern about the industry’s reputation and called for greater regulation and tougher advertising rules.
A total of 8 hospitals in Malaysia have obtained the JCI international medical certification in the United States.
South Korea is known as a "plastic surgery paradise".
The cost of treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals was a three-fold increase from 52.5 billion won in 2012.
The heat of the plastic surgery industry in South Korea has not decreased under the impact of the third wave of the epidemic.
Ms. Park, 50, had a series of postoperative infections after plastic surgery, resulting in her right chest being half the size of her left chest.
The average growth rate of the medical tourism sector in Malaysia is as high as 100% in the past 5 years.
The revenue of plastic surgery in South Korea increased by 10% year-on-year from January to October last year.
The most popular medical services in Malaysia include cosmetic surgery, dentistry and dermatology.
In South Korea, plastic surgery is as common as a haircut.
Malaysia has won the title of the world's best medical country for the third consecutive year.
Nearly 50,000 foreigners visited South Korea for cosmetic surgery last year.
Malaysian doctors can master fluent English.
Many people who want to take the opportunity to have a cosmetic surgery have found that the longer the people who book a cosmetic consultation, the longer the time to schedule an operation.
There is no need to keep patients waiting too long in Malaysia.
Foreigners receiving treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals accounted for 12.3% of all foreign patients.
The medical tourism sector in Malaysia has grown vigorously in the past 10 years.
Many hospitals in Malaysia are certified in Western countries.
More and more foreigners are seeking medical treatment in Malaysia.
The goal of allowing citizens to go out 500 meters to see a 3,000 square meter park green space has been basically achieved.
Another "pocket square" will be arranged in the courtyard of the house on Lyatoshinsky Street, 26-b in the Goloseevsky district of the capital.
There are 243 urban parks in Shanghai, and by 2020, the number of urban parks will reach 300, forming a three-level system of "pocket parks - community parks - regional parks".
The project will create urban pocket parks and improve the open space
The per capita public green space in Shanghai is only about 8%, while some cities in China are generally more than 10%.
The project aims to improve the quality of life of residents
The presence of pocket parks allows nearby residents to walk, chat, fitness, and enjoy the green, and also lights up the environmental face of the block.
Shanghai is still far behind Beijing, Hangzhou, Guangzhou, and other cities, as well as New York, London, and Paris in terms of public green space.
The municipal association "Kievzelenstroy" offers to solve the problem by creating "house" or "pocket" squares.
Pocket Park is not clearly defined, but refers to small green open spaces scattered in plaques in the city.
The Huishan Garden at 207 Lintong Road has an area of only 408 square meters, but has exquisite and small green products, and fitness trails.
The city built 47 pocket parks last year, and this year will build or upgrade 50.
Putuo plans to build a new 10,000 li community green space with a total area of 14,930 square meters, consisting of forests, wetlands, and pocket parks.
Among the 79 colorful community projects recently released in Pudong, nearly 40 projects are defined as pocket parks.
Large parks in megacities are often far away and not always convenient to get to.
The idea of creating pocket squares came from colleagues from Poland.
The city's greening department defines the street garden from the area generally as the area of 300-3000 square meters of green space.
3 million yuan of funds will be sought to open up the Pocket Park in Tamsui District.
The media monitoring center at the Press and Information Agency of Uzbekistan concluded that the series contradicts national mentality and traditions, as well as family values of Uzbeks.
“Magnificent Century ”, an epic drama about Sulaiman, the Ottoman monarch, attracted more than 500 million viewers worldwide in 2013.
Saudi Arabia wants to export its cultural influence through the film and television industry and break Turkey ’ s cultural influence in the region.
Turkish television series have reached South America after capturing the film market of the Indian subcontinent, the Middle East, the Caucasus, Central Asia and the Balkans.
Some critics discredited the series as a "propaganda" series.
The series “Bride of Istanbul” is an example of Turkey’s successful series, produced in the “Telenovela” genre.
Saudi Arabia has actively developed the film and television industry, and even cooperated with the United Arab Emirates in order to “fight ” with Turkey in the field of television.
Erdogan took advantage of poverty and the civil war in Somalia to solidify his influence by controlling the economic and military aspects of the country.
Turkish TV series are the most popular among all series in Uzbekistan, having overtaken both Korean and local Uzbek series.
Critics say the series has an "informational" function.
The US $40 million series Kingdoms of Fire, jointly funded by the two governments, was broadcast in 2019, highlighting the Ottoman Empire as the tyrant who has ravaged the Arab world for centuries.
Top military officials of the Arab Republic of Egypt, Israel, and Sudan will hold a meeting to discuss security coordination in the Red Sea region and plans to limit Turkish influence in the region.
In 2018, the Saudi government and MBC stopped buying Turkish drama, describing “Noor ” as “anti-Islamic ” and “subversive ”.
TV critic Sina Kluoğlu in the Milliat newspaper added: "Advertising in this type of series is natural. The point is that it should be done cautiously."
The series “Bride of Istanbul” reflects the controversial contrast of modern values with conservative traditions.
The imam-khatib of the cathedral mosque "Mirza Yusuf" of the Yunusabad district of Tashkent, Rahmatullo Saifuddinov, said that Turkish series adversely affect the gene pool of the nation.
Egypt banned Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan for “revenge ” after he backed then-President Mohamed Morsi during Egypt ’ s 2013 military coup.
The popularity of Turkish series in the Middle East or the Caucasus may be due to cultural and historical commonalities.
Okor added, “We have to tell our stories more gently, otherwise the TV won’t play it.”
The series “Bride of Istanbul” will be broadcast on Star Channel in Turkey and Viva Channel in Israel.

The MBC network, which is based in the United Arab Emirates and is founded by a Saudi capitalist, introduces the Ottoman government as an occupying force in a series on the overthrow of the Mamluk government.
Turkey has contributed more than 150 TV series to an estimated 500 million viewers in 146 countries around the world.
According to an analysis by social media outlet Khuros, more than 620,000 tweets with the hashtag were posted between the first episode of the series in March and the last episode in June.
Support for the Muslim Brotherhood and other Islamic movements remains a major controversy in both Turkey and Saudi Arabia.
A stream of spectacular Turkish television projects was shown in Russia after the success of “The Magnificent Century ”.
Expanding the clumsy imitation and lack of content of the series can annoy the audience and lead the home show to oblivion, making the ruling management of this space essential.
In Russia, it is proposed to ban the broadcast of Turkish TV series and films on television.
More than 150 Turkish series have been exported to more than 100 countries, according to the Turkish Ministry of Culture and Tourism.
The ban on the Turkish TV series "Black Love" sparked a flurry of outraged comments on social media.
Relations between Turkey and Saudi Arabia have deteriorated to the point that even Saudi Arabia’s MBC TV group, which broadcasts Turkish series in Saudi Arabia, stopped broadcasting them last year.
Turkish TV series has been broadcast as the country’s soft power in recent years, especially on the television of Arab countries.
“The Magnificent Century ” made an impression on sophisticated spectators with its intriguing plot, bright play of actors, spectacular outfits and decorations, and sincere feelings.
Turkey’s addiction to the Turkish series has also reached Israel.
Turkish TV stars have fans in Russia.
The Perseverance rover has a device for obtaining oxygen from the Martian atmosphere.
The Mars oxygen field resource utilization experimental instrument, MOXIE, makes 5 grams of oxygen from carbon dioxide in the Martian atmosphere.
MOXIE produced five grams of oxygen to allow astronauts to breathe for about 10 minutes.
The Martian atmosphere is dominated by carbon dioxide, accounting for 96%, and oxygen only 0.13%.
The technology for obtaining oxygen from the Martian atmosphere will be used to obtain rocket fuel needed during manned missions to Mars.

A test vehicle for extracting oxygen from the Martian atmosphere, as well as a "space helicopter" that is set to fly for the first time on a planet other than Earth, are among those on the Perseverance rover.
MOXIE converts carbon dioxide into oxygen and carbon monoxide (CO)
MOXIE can obtain up to 10 grams of oxygen per hour
The US Space Agency (NASA) successfully converted carbon dioxide into oxygen in experiments on Mars.
The Mars Oxygen In-situ Resource Utilization Experiment (MOXIE) on the perseverance spacecraft will try to convert Martian carbon dioxide into oxygen.
The device also produces 25 times more oxygen than NASA ’ s Mars Oxygen ISRU Experiment in July.
The Mars Oxygen In-situ Resource Utilization Experiment, or MOXIE, produced about five grams of oxygen in an hour.
Maxi takes Mars’ resources and processes them into usable materials such as propellants, breathable air, or combined with hydrogen into water.
The conversion of carbon dioxide into oxygen on Mars is the first time in human history that it has achieved a new milestone on a planet other than Earth.
Future versions of the technology could work on Mars to produce breathable oxygen.
Oxygen production from Mars ’ atmosphere took place on Tuesday, just one day after genius ’ s helicopter flight.
Analysts estimate that the settlement could eventually cost Bayer between $8 billion and $12 billion.
Bayer has settled 88,500 of 125,000 claims.
German pharmaceutical company Bayer said its U.S. company Monsanto production of herbicide "Roundup" reported cases of cancer in the United States in the second quarter of about 5,000 new cases.
Since Bayer acquired Monsanto for US $63 billion, it has faced about 18,400 lawsuits in the United States.
Monsanto has been sued in the United States for suspected cancer caused by the herbicide Roundup, and Bayer is seeking to settle the lawsuit with $11 billion in damages.
The court ordered Bayer to pay the plaintiffs $86.7 million instead of the previous $2 billion
Bayer has been accused by farmers of causing cancer by the Roundup herbicide product.

Bayer AG will announce that it has reached an approximately $10.5 billion agreement with plaintiffs in the United States who have accused its Roundup herbicide of causing cancer to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits.
Bayer increased the cost of future claims to $2 billion last week.
An agreement to settle a large number of lawsuits was terminated earlier this month.
Bayer has been sued by 52,500 plaintiffs in the United States because the herbicide glyphosate is suspected to cause cancer.
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
Bayer has reserved funds to settle claims for the potentially carcinogenic herbicide Roundup in the amount of $10.9 billion
Bayer will pay about $9.5 billion to settle claims filed by lawyers representing some 95,000 plaintiffs.
Bayer announced plans in June to settle all legal lawsuits against Monsanto for $12 billion.
Bayer AG made additional provisions in the third quarter to cover the increased costs associated with the case.
More than 13,400 similar lawsuits against Bayer have been filed in the US courts
A court in the United States already found Bayer responsible for the fact that the herbicide produced by Monsanto caused cancer in a married couple.
Bayer’s $10 billion legal battle settlement plan aims to end legal disputes following its acquisition of Monsanto in 2018.
The evidence shows that, despite Monsanto’s knowledge that the Roundup insecticide may pose a cancer risk to the human body, the company sought to downplay concerns about the safety of its products.
San Francisco federal judge dismisses Monsanto Co. Proposed $2 billion settlement.
Bayer completed its acquisition of Monsanto in 2018, settling nearly 100,000 cancer lawsuits in the United States in June at a sky-high price of $10.9 billion.
Bayer will set aside $8 billion to resolve all current cases, including those pending.
The jury decided that Monsanto violated the law by not warning buyers about the likely risks of using Roundup, and ordered the company to pay a fine of $2 billion and $55 million in damages.
The claimants have not yet formally agreed to accept this compensation
The agreement was made with an estimated 50,000 to 85,000 plaintiffs.
Police are questioning the two men, who appear to be members of a large gang, after the local newspaper Libertà received a confession from the two men about the 1997 robbery.
In the Italian city of Piacenza a picture of the Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a woman ” was found, the cost of which is estimated at 60 million euros.
Workers found the painting in a plastic bag hidden in the wall of the Ricci Oddi Gallery, where it was stolen in 1997.
The painting was painted by Klimt in 1916-1917.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.
Gustav Klimt ’ s Portrait of a Woman is on the list of the world ’ s most wanted works.
Workers cleaned the gallery building of ivy and found the painting in a black bag in the partition between the outer walls of the gallery
Lawyer Gibo Guglieri told the publication that the thieves allegedly returned the job four years ago.
The Ricci-Oddi Museum of Modern Art in Italy has discovered a painting by Austrian painter Gustav Klimt that was stolen 23 years ago, worth 60 million euros.
A gardener at the Ricci Oddi Gallery found a plastic bag containing the famous painting inside the sandwich of the wall in December last year.
The thieves said in a letter to Ermanno Marian, an employee of the newspaper Libertà, that they stole the work of the Austrian artist worth 60 million euros in February 1997, and then returned it.

In the Italian city of Piacenza, a gardener peeled ivy off the wall in the Ricci Oddi gallery and accidentally found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman ” stolen 22 years ago from the same gallery.
The thieves kept quiet about the details, but insisted that the work was not always in the niche.
The painting was discovered at the scene of the robbery.
The canvas was handed over to the police.
The painting was found inside the crack in a wall in the gallery.
The painting was found in a groove in the museum’s exterior wall.
Global warming has already had an impact on agricultural production.
Modifying the pattern of cultivation and development of low-water crops can reduce water consumption in the farms and gardens of Qazvin province
Drought has affected more than 110,000 hectares of agricultural land in the province
The agricultural sector continues to face a drought water crisis due to increased temperature and less than normal rainfall.

Climatic models show that since the beginning of October in many provinces of the eastern half of the country, such as North Khorasan, South Khorasan, Razavi Khorasan, Semnan, Golestan, Kerman, Isfahan, Yazd, Sistan and Baluchestan, Hormozgan, Fars and even Bushehr have been 50 to 90 percent less than normal rainfall.
Climate change has changed the favorable conditions for growing crops and is hurting the food chain in the future.
Plans have been made to reduce the level of cultivation of high-water crops every year and replace them with other crops that have economic justification for farmers.
Drought reduces 35% of North Khorasan ’ s garden crops
Climate change has affected all three climatic regions of Mazandaran and has had negative consequences for agricultural species and other plants.
In Iran, the average temperature in the country is increasing.
Climate change is a threat to food production in Ukraine.
The lands are being plowed and sown with crops that are more unpretentious to lack of moisture.
Drought is expected to reduce the harvest of saffron crops in the province
This year the climate has changed and continuous rainfall and temperature reduction have affected the production cycle of the rice crop in Mazandaran.
Farmers need to take into account climate change and adapt to it.
The area under cultivation of crops such as cantaloupe or cotton in the region has been reduced from 2,000 hectares to 300 hectares.
The increase in temperature has caused behavioral changes in the field of agriculture.
Climate change has caused the snowfall in the winter of Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari Province to change to rain.
Rapid global climate change has seriously affected the growth of crops, including warming and changes in rainfall patterns.
72.6% of the total one million hectares of agricultural and horticultural land of Khorasan Razavi is under the cultivation of a variety of crops by "water method".
The rainfall of Isfahan province decreased by 38% in the current agricultural year
Crops such as melons, bamboo shoots and golden needles are obviously affected by climate change.

The effects of low rainfall in the western part of the country are 15 to 40 percent, according to the rainfall statistics in Kermanshah 42 percent, Kurdistan 19 percent, East Azerbaijan 14 percent, West Azerbaijan 13 percent, Ardebil 8 percent, Alborz 10 percent and Tehran 15 percent less than normal.
Unpredictable temperature fluctuations can lead to crop declines.
Farmers should change their management of cultivation.
Drought and water shortage caused 35% of the area under cultivation of agricultural products in the province to be reduced.

Climate change and change in the time of rainfall and temperature causes the drying of pasture plants, which increases the crises of flood, dust with tectonic origin, increase environmental hazards and increase the costs of compensation.
Human activity influences precipitation intensity.
Climate change can affect the crops and products of various crops, plants, animals and so on.
There are prerequisites for a high harvest.
The rapid pace of climate change is causing problems.
Water restrictions exist in the region's agricultural sector.
Farmers should benefit from rainfall.
Drought has reduced the harvest of gardeners ’ crops in the province this year
The Golden Bear for Best Film of the 70th Berlinale goes to "Sheytan vojud nadarad" (There Is No Evil), directed by Mohammad Rasoulof.
The film won an Amnesty International award.
Mohammad Rasoulof was not allowed to leave Iran and still won with the film "The Devil Does Not Exist".
The film was present in the Berlin competition section and was the only representative of Iranian cinema at this year ’ s Berlin Film Festival.
The film "There Is No Evil", directed by Iranian dissident director Mohammad Rasoulof, won the Golden Bear Award for Best Picture at the Berlin Film Festival.
The film explores our responsibilities and choices in life.
Innocence, an Iranian film, is about the death penalty.
The festival is regarded as the most political among the prestigious film festivals around the world
Jafar Panahi's film "Offside" won several awards, including the Silver Bear of the Berlin Festival.
The film was shot outside of Iran.
Iranian cinema had won the Berlin Golden Bear with the film “A Separation from Simin ” by Asghar Farhadi.
Iranian film Innocence wins Golden Bear at Berlin Film Festival
The film won the Golden Bear at the Berlin International Film Festival in 2017 and brought the director to fame.
This film narrates the true story of a person named Hossein Sabzian who introduces himself as a well-known director.
Iran has won two of the best international films or best foreign language films in the last ten Oscars, which are 2012 ’ s A Separation and 2017 ’ s The Salesman, both from director Asghar Farhadi.
German film critics called Jude's film "an insane movie that questions politics and ethics and gets the Golden Bear for its courage".
Mohammad Rasoulof is an Iranian dissident filmmaker who was banned by the government.

Iranian filmmakers such as Sohrab Shahid-Sales and Asghar Farhadi received Silver Bear and Golden Bear at the Berlin Film Festival for Dead Nature and Nader and Simin ’ s Divorce in 1974 and 2011, respectively.
Nearly 4,000 people have crossed the English Channel this year in more than 300 small boats

Home Secretary Sajid David cut short his Christmas holiday with his family in Africa on Sunday and returned home to tackle a difficult situation with an unexpected wave of illegal migrants arriving in Britain by boat from France.
British officials confirmed that the 17 migrants, including three children, were able to reach British shores by hijacking a 12-meter fishing boat near the French port of Calais early Tuesday.
A significant number of the migrants attempting to cross the English Channel are Iranians, Afghans and Syrians
France and the United Kingdom have signed a new agreement to end illegal immigration through the English Channel.
A growing number of migrants have attempted to cross the dangerous and busy transport route from southern France to the south of Britain, killing four people last year and seven so far this year.
British Prime Minister Boris Johnson warned refugees not to cross the Channel on Friday, saying "we will send you back" and that the law would treat them as "illegal immigrants".
235 illegal migrants in 17 boats attempting to cross the English Channel from northern France were intercepted by British Coast Guard and Border Guard officers on Thursday.
There were 49 immigrants from Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan, including one minor.
French authorities intercepted 19 migrants trying to sneak into Britain in the English Channel yesterday night.
Refugees from the Philippines, Nigeria and Guinea have also attempted to cross the Channel by boat in recent months.

The Minister of immigration control of Great Britain Chris Philp spoke about the intention of the government to stop the flow of refugees and return to France those who have already managed to cross the English Channel.
The Maritime Prefecture of the English Channel and the North Sea announced the rescue of 45 migrants who tried to illegally get to England on board a homemade boat.
None of the migrants were injured
Last month alone, more than 140 people attempted to cross the English Channel.
The UK made changes to the immigration system to make it harder for people who enter the country illegally.
British officials said on Monday that the coast guard had handed over 36 asylum-seekers who were trying to cross the English Channel on three separate boats.
The English Channel, between France and Britain, is one of the world ’ s busiest waterways, and it can be dangerous to cross by small vessels, especially in winter when the sea is stormy.
Britain's Home Secretary called the rise in the number of Channel migrants a 'serious accident'

Iran's killing of Ukraine Flight 752 was a terrorist act, according to a Canadian court ruling.
